episodes.title: Have You Ever Been In A Cult? We Have - S2 Ep 8 - PODCAST
episodes.description:
We’re your hosts, Heather and Desiree, two millennial late bloomers consistently behind in life, talking about it all: love, loss, trauma, spirituality, books, and much more.

With current events unfolding in the United States, the topic of Cults has come back around. This episode explores 6 characteristics of cults, how organized religion can sometimes mirror cult-like traits, and the importance of self-awareness and critical thinking in spiritual and community involvement.

Be sure to head over to the comments section and answer our CALL TO ACTION questions from the end of today’s episode.

Chapters
00:00 The Cult Phenomenon Resurfaces
04:23 Recognizing Cult-like Traits in Religion
09:18 The Beloved Leader and Accountability
14:52 Group Theology and Elitism
24:50 The Desire for Belonging and Legacy
26:06 The Pursuit of Purpose and Greatness
27:18 Questioning and Rebellion in Belief Systems
29:47 The Role of Guilt and Fear in Control
31:38 Political and Religious Parallels in Questioning
33:23 Punishment and Isolation Tactics
35:19 Attacks on Morality and Character
37:05 The Collective vs. Individual Identity
39:00 Isolation and Community Dynamics
41:52 Fear and Control Mechanisms
44:31 The Illusion of Choice and Sacrifice
47:29 The Cost of Belonging and Exploitation
53:09 The Church as a Lifestyle
56:33 Isolation and Control in Religious Communities
59:12 The Struggle for Individual Identity
01:02:23 The Fear of Leaving and the Cost of Belonging
01:04:45 The Collective vs. Individual Value
01:08:43 The Cycle of Shame and Guilt
01:12:23 Finding Peace After Leaving
01:15:19 The Importance of Self-Care and Boundaries
